sources.label: Edison report on Nabaltec (Jun 1, 2026) - flame-retardant fillers for data cables, battery thermal management
Nabaltec Q1 2026 figures (May 21) - gradual return to revenue growth expected from Q2
KunststoffWeb - Nabaltec FY26 guidance 4-6% revenue growth, data-center cable demand cited

verdicts.rankRationale: No material news since June 6; today's -0.87% is drift and the stock remains down ~8% YTD with the latent ceramic-fillers thesis unchanged, so the strict-screen setup is exactly where yesterday's hold left it.
verdicts.bull: Edison and management both flag data-center fire-safe cabling, EV charging and battery thermal management as demand drivers into a guided 4-6% FY26 revenue recovery; at ~$120M market cap and negative YTD, the name is genuinely under-owned with the AI hook still unpriced.
verdicts.neutral: Q1 2026 showed revenue -2.7% and EBIT -34% with Specialty Aluminas still -4.6%; the recovery is guided, not delivered, so the latent option stays cheap but unproven until Q2 prints.
verdicts.bear: This is a cyclical specialty-chemicals micro-cap whose AI exposure is indirect filler content in cables and enclosures; a stalled European industrial recovery or continued alumina weakness keeps margins compressed regardless of AI buildout.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Q2 2026 results confirming the guided return to revenue growth, with visible data-center/fire-safety cable demand in Functional Fillers.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Recovery slips again (third consecutive guide-down risk) and EBIT margin stays below the 5-7% target, invalidating the trough call.
